findings.evidence: Actual child marker exists after broker crash-before-start-event, while replay reports gemini_process_starts=0; before-spawn and after-spawn ledgers are byte-identical.
findings.risk: A real Gemini process can be misreported as zero, enabling unsafe recovery or replay.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a durable fork/exec handshake and complete/blocked/ambiguous replay statuses; never infer zero across the ambiguous crash window.
findings.validation_gap: No real broker crash-before/after-spawn probes exist in candidate tests.

findings.evidence: Out-of-order terminal/start, a re-chained duplicate OPERATION_CREATED, and invalid outcome/ordinal all replay complete.
findings.risk: Malformed or contradictory ledgers can be certified complete.
findings.suggested_fix: Validate event-specific schemas and the full ordered finite-state machine.
findings.validation_gap: Tests cover exact duplicate and partial tail only, not semantically invalid valid-hash chains.

findings.evidence: The fake Gemini is launched by the parent, not broker; broker FD remains inheritable; wrong close_fds/pass_fds and a foreign inherited FD leak in fresh probes.
findings.risk: Option B may grant writer authority to the Gemini child or another inherited descriptor despite a green matrix.
findings.suggested_fix: Run the fake target from the real broker with a strict FD allowlist/CLOEXEC policy and adversarial negative controls.

findings.evidence: A same-UID self-consistent full ledger replacement replays complete without an old external anchor and is detected only when that anchor is supplied.
findings.risk: After parent/broker crash, ledger self-authentication can hide a rewritten history.
findings.suggested_fix: Specify an independently trusted anchor owner and crash lifecycle, or mark full same-UID replacement OUT_OF_SCOPE.
findings.validation_gap: No anchor-loss or full-chain-replacement test exists.

findings.evidence: EXEC_FAILURE is absent; preflight missing and a real Popen FileNotFoundError produce byte-identical complete ledgers.
findings.risk: The ledger cannot distinguish preflight zero from an exec-boundary failure or race.
findings.suggested_fix: Separate preflight rejection, fork attempt, exec failure, process start, and terminal events with explicit accounting semantics.
findings.validation_gap: No actual missing executable or executable race test exists.

findings.evidence: All matrix statuses are literal tuples and have no observable/predicate linkage.
findings.risk: The matrix remains green even if runtime behavior regresses.
findings.suggested_fix: Derive every supported cell from a referenced observable and negative-control predicate.
findings.validation_gap: No mutation test proves status changes when an observable fails.

findings.evidence: The proposed API and allowlist omit broker framing, FD policy, anchor ownership, exec handshake, exact caller/callsite, and legacy retry cutover.
findings.risk: A production implementer must invent security-critical architecture beyond a bounded card.
findings.suggested_fix: Lock the wire/API contract and exact caller migration surface before implementation dispatch.
findings.validation_gap: No caller-level fake executable trace demonstrates an unambiguous 0/1 integration.
